title: Remove diagnostic API and fix NIF module derivation
purpose: run_until_exit_for_diagnostic is a public blocking method on RuntimeHandle left over from E2E debugging. registered_nif_modules() is a hardcoded list. Both need cleanup for production readiness.
task: Remove or gate run_until_exit_for_diagnostic behind #[cfg(test)]. Restore start.rs to use spawn_workflow_with_policy. Derive registered_nif_modules from the actual NifRegistration entries instead of hardcoding.

requirements.title: Remove diagnostic blocking API
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose run_until_exit_for_diagnostic as a public method in non-test builds. THE SYSTEM SHALL gate it behind #[cfg(test)] or remove it entirely.
acceptance: run_until_exit_for_diagnostic is not callable from aion-server or any non-test code.
start.rs uses spawn_workflow_with_policy (trap_exit enabled) as designed.
No eprintln debug output in lifecycle or engine_nifs code.

requirements.title: Derive NIF module exclusion from registration
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL derive the NIF-backed module names from the NifRegistration entries (the Mfa module field), not from a hardcoded list in engine_nifs.rs.
acceptance: registered_nif_modules returns module names extracted from the actual NIF entries.
Adding a new engine NIF automatically excludes its module from content-hash renaming.
No hardcoded module name strings in nif_module_names().

boundaries: SHALL NOT remove the take_exit_error/take_exit_exception diagnostic capability — those are valuable for test code.
SHALL NOT change the NIF registration mechanism — only how module names are extracted from it.
verification: No eprintln in non-test code paths.
grep for hardcoded 'aion_flow_ffi' in nif_module_names shows zero results.
